benchmark_design.locked_holdout_policy: Holdout cases are generated from a fixed seed after train/validation and are used only for final scoring, release comparison, negative controls, and public receipts.

large_agent_coordination.coordination_layer: Capability Transfer Atlas: a large-scale autonomous specialist-agent coordination lattice for converting work traces into reusable cross-domain skill releases.

mechanism: demand → decomposition → capability atlas → specialist-agent market clearing → trace distillation → verifier courts → locked-domain transfer → release selection → routing policy → reinvestment → compounding generalization
public_safe_claim: SkillOS does not claim achieved superintelligence, live revenue, investment advice, legal advice, policy advice, token recommendations, or Kardashev Type II civilization. It makes the transfer mechanism publicly runnable: can a large autonomous specialist-agent organization turn verified traces into reusable skills that improve future work across unseen domains?
title: Autonomous RSI Cross-Domain Capability Transfer Atlas Proof

verification_gates.detail: locked holdout has thousands of cross-domain cases
verification_gates.name: large specialist-agent coordination

verification_gates.detail: SkillOS RSI materially outperforms static skills on unseen domains
verification_gates.name: transfer beats static skill catalog

verification_gates.detail: shuffled traces and verifier-free releases cannot pass the proof
verification_gates.name: negative controls fail

verification_gates.detail: selected release has zero benchmark risk breaches and zero unauthorized actions
verification_gates.name: risk discipline

verification_gates.detail: later validation-gated releases improve locked-domain transfer
verification_gates.name: RSI release selection

verification_gates.detail: all outputs are generated by local Python scripts with no network call and no private data
verification_gates.name: public reproducibility

why_next_best: After proving capability liquidity and proof-forge meta-coordination, the next critical test is external validity: whether released skills transfer to new domains instead of overfitting to a single demo.
